text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
Our senior-year summer has come to<n>an end—one
I spent running around<n>with Matikanefukukitaru.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Wow, we really gave 110 percent out<n>there, huh,
Trainer!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
With my time at summer camp and<n>the regional
races, I've had lots of<n>opportunities to take in the
wonders<n>of nature!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
What I really want to say is, gods<n>exist in all kinds
of different places.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Hm, I don't know how best to put it,<n>but gods
exist in every mountain,<n>every ocean, and every
town...

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
By connecting with them, I feel more<n>and more
energized.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Oh, but that doesn't just apply to<n>gods. I think it
helps me to connect<n>with other people too.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
People exist in all corners of this<n>world, most of
them strangers. By<n>trusting in them and having
them do<n>the same in return, we can all lift our
spirits together.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
That's what faith is all about—the<n>power of
believing.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Whoa, that sounded pretty deep! I<n>can't believe I
just said that!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
I don't know if I'm making any<n>sense, but never
mind that. Let's<n>head back home! The Arima Kinen
is<n>waiting for us!
